name: Geryon

visual_attributes.morphology: humanoid giant with multiple bodies or heads
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: three bodies joined at the waist, each with its own head and limbs

domain_specific_visual.WARRIOR_ARMOR: wears three helmets and carries three shields and spears
domain_specific_visual.WINGED: possesses wings as depicted in some accounts

domain_specific_functional.GUARDIAN_OF_CATTLE: owned a herd of magnificent red cattle
domain_specific_functional.ENEMY_OF_HERACLES: defeated by Heracles during his tenth labor

relationships.description: Geryon is the son of Chrysaor
Geryon is the son of Callirrhoe
Geryon is the grandson of Medusa
Geryon is the nephew of Pegasus
Geryon was defeated by Heracles during his tenth labor

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: far west of the Mediterranean, associated with Tartessos in southern Iberia
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents a formidable challenge in Heracles' labors, symbolizing strength and guardianship

contrastive_constraints: NOT a generic warrior without multiple heads or bodies
